behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file paths, function names (jsonlStreamConsumer, createStreamsManager), expected error message literal 'Stream closed before head was received', and expected values [0,1,2,3,4].

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug (controller.error discards chunks) and expected behavior, but doesn't dictate the specific code fix; the developer must figure out how to restructure handlers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests only check behavior (values received and error message/type), not internal function names like handleClose/closeAll; alternative implementations would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests spawn tsx subprocesses that import and call actual jsonl functions, asserting on runtime behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual stream plumbing to work — a stub couldn't deliver [0,1,2,3,4] through jsonlStreamProducer/Consumer nor throw the specific error message.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Both symptoms (data loss on normal completion, non-descriptive error on premature close) are covered by the two f2p tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings explaining F2P/P2P classification.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to the module file but not the exact line; localization within the file remains part of the task.
